word_list.word: a stumble may prevent a fall
word_list.phonetic_text: ə stəm.bəl meɪ prɪ.vɛnt ə fɑ:l
word_list.phonetic_am_text: ē stēm.bēl mei pri.vent ē faal
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ stˈʌmbəl mˈeɪ pɹɪvˈɛnt ɐ fˈɔːl

senses.definition: used to suggest that a small mistake or setback can serve as a warning or wake-up call that helps one avoid a larger, more serious problem or failure down the line

word_list.word: an ounce of (prevention|protection) is worth a pound of cure
word_list.pronunciation: ɐn ˈaʊns ʌv pɹɪvˈɛnʃən ɔːɹ pɹətˈɛkʃən ɪz wˈɜːθ ɐ pˈaʊnd ʌv kjˈʊɹ

senses.definition: used to imply that it is better to take steps to prevent problems from happening in the first place, rather than waiting to deal with the consequences of those problems later
used to suggest that it is preferable to take precautions and be cautious in order to avoid problems or regret later on

examples.x: Better safe than sorry, so I wore a helmet while biking, even though I wasn’t going fast.
We bought a backup generator, better safe than sorry, since the storm was coming.
We arrived early for the concert, better safe than sorry, to avoid missing the opening act.

word_list.word: it is easier to raise the devil than to lay him
word_list.pronunciation: ɪt ɪz ˈiːzɪɚ tə ɹˈeɪz ðə dˈɛvəl ðɐn tə lˈeɪ hˌɪm

senses.definition: used to imply that it is easier to create a problem or cause trouble than it is to solve it or correct the situation
used to imply that it is better to be overly cautious and prevent issues from arising than having to deal with the consequences later

word_list.pronunciation: pɹɪvˈɛnʃən ɪz ˈɔːlweɪz ɔːɹ bˈɛɾɚ ðɐn kjˈʊɹ

senses.definition: used to imply that it is more effective to take preventative measures to avoid problems or illnesses before they occur, rather than waiting to deal with them after they have already happened

examples.x: Regular exercise and healthy eating habits can prevent many illnesses as prevention is better than cure.

senses.definition: used to suggest that securely binding or storing something ensures its safe retrieval later, emphasizing the importance of taking precautions and being organized

word_list.word: a little fire is quickly trodden out
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ lˈɪɾəl fˈaɪɚɹ ɪz kwˈɪkli tɹˈɑːdən ˈaʊt

senses.definition: used to emphasize that it is important to deal with issues as soon as they arise, before they have a chance to grow and become more difficult to handle

examples.x: The manager knew that a little fire is quickly trodden out, so she quickly addressed the issue before it had a chance to escalate.

word_list.word: never trouble trouble (till|until) trouble troubles you
word_list.pronunciation: nˈɛvɚ tɹˈʌbəl tɹˈʌbəl tˈɪl ɔːɹ ʌntˈɪl tɹˈʌbəl tɹˈʌbəlz juː

senses.definition: used to advise against being overly anxious or worrying about potential problems that have not yet arisen and suggests that one should only deal with problems when they actually occur
